title: First Ever Bill to Ignore Roe and Abolish Abortion in Colorado: HB22-1079
author: James Silberman
orientation: Announces the first Colorado abolition bill (HB22-1079) sponsored by Rep. Dave Williams, which would recognize personhood from fertilization, extend equal-protection homicide laws to the preborn, and treat Roe v. Wade as void within the state. The article frames the bill as a long-shot under a Democrat-controlled legislature but a culturally significant first move that the church should rally around.

questions.question: What does HB22-1079 actually do, and who introduced it?
questions.answer: It is the first Colorado bill to immediately and totally abolish abortion in defiance of Roe v. Wade. The prime sponsor is Rep. Dave Williams, joined by Reps. Kim Ransom, Ron Hanks, and Shane Sandridge as co-sponsors.
questions.quote: For the first time, a bill to totally and immediately abolish abortion in defiance of *Roe v. Wade* has been filed in the Centennial State: [HB22-1079, An Act Concerning Abolishing Abortion in Colorado](https://freethestates.org/leg.colorado.gov/sites/default/files/documents/2022A/bills/2022a_1079_01.pdf). The bill's prime sponsor is Rep. Dave Williams, with Reps. Kim Ransom, Ron Hanks, and Shane Sandridge also sponsoring the bill.

questions.question: On what theological and legal grounds does the bill claim authority to recognize the personhood of the preborn?
questions.answer: The legislative declaration grounds the bill in the sanctity of innocent human life created in the image of God, asserting that personhood begins at fertilization and that all unborn children deserve equal protection prior to birth.
questions.quote:
(1) The general assembly finds and declares, acknowledging the sanctity of innocent human life created in the image of God, which should be equally protected from fertilization to natural death, that the purpose of this act is:*

*(a) To fully recognize the human personhood of an unborn child at all stages of development prior to birth from the moment of fertilization;

questions.question: How does HB22-1079 propose to handle Roe v. Wade and other federal actions that conflict with it?
questions.answer: The bill instructs the general assembly to treat as void any federal statute, regulation, treaty, executive order, or court ruling that strips an unborn child of the right to life or denies equal protection of that right. In effect, it nullifies Roe within Colorado.
questions.quote: (2) Therefore, the general assembly intends to treat as void and give no effect to any and all federal statutes, regulations, treaties, orders, and court rulings that deprive an unborn child of the right to life or prohibit the equal protection of such right.

questions.question: What enforcement mechanism does the bill apply to state judges who would block its protections?
questions.answer: The bill makes any state judge who violates the right to life subject to impeachment, putting judicial actors on notice that they cannot quietly nullify the statute from the bench.
questions.quote: The bill establishes full legal equality for preborn children, nullifies *Roe v. Wade* and any other federal action that strips preborn children of their rights, and establishes that any state judge who violates the right to life is subject to impeachment.

questions.question: Given Colorado's political landscape, what are the bill's realistic chances of passage and why file it anyway?
questions.answer: Silberman concedes the bill faces an uphill battle because Democrats control both chambers and the governorship, but argues that simply getting an abolition bill introduced is itself a major step toward shifting the political and cultural terrain of the state.
questions.quote: HB22-1079 faces an uphill battle in Colorado which is controlled by substantial Democrat majorities in both chambers of the legislature and has a Democrat governor. Still, getting an abolition bill introduced is a huge first step toward shifting the politics and culture of a state.

questions.question: What does the article ask Colorado pastors and churches to do in response to this bill?
questions.answer: It calls Colorado churches, which the author says have failed to be salt and light, to rally around the bill, pray, and act as public ambassadors for Christ who love their preborn neighbors.
questions.quote: The churches in our nation have failed to be salt and light. Now Colorado abolitionists have something tangible to call Christians in Colorado to rally around. Be in prayer for the churches in Colorado, that they would be the public ambassadors for Christ and love their preborn neighbors, pushing back darkness in their state.

related.title: State Rep. Bryan Slaton Files Bill to Immediately Abolish Abortion in Texas
related.reason: Parallel state-level abolition bill from the same author one year earlier; useful for comparing Texas and Colorado strategies.

related.title: State Rep. Chris Kurka to File Bill to Immediately Abolish Abortion in Alaska
related.reason: Another contemporaneous immediate-abolition bill in a different state, showing the broader legislative pattern HB22-1079 belongs to.

related.title: Reps. Curt Nisly, John Jacob Author the Bill to Abolish Abortion in Indiana
related.reason: Indiana's equivalent personhood-from-conception bill; complements Colorado's HB22-1079 as part of the same multi-state push.

related.title: AZ Rep. Walt Blackman Presents Abolition of Abortion in Arizona Act to 1,200+ Supporters
related.reason: Neighboring-state abolition bill announced just days before HB22-1079 was filed; useful regional context for Mountain West abolitionism.

related.title: Bill to Abolish Abortion in South Carolina Filed by State Rep. Jonathan Hill
related.reason: Another immediate-abolition state bill with similar equal-protection framing to HB22-1079.

related.title: Abortion Abolition Bill Introduced in Washington
related.reason: Earlier example of an abolition bill filed in a Democrat-controlled state, directly relevant to Silberman's argument that filing matters even when passage is unlikely.

related.title: Missouri Abolition Bill Author Mike Moon Elected to State Senate
related.reason: Profile of another abolition-bill sponsor; useful for readers tracking the network of legislators behind these state bills.
